Definition: Unique patient identifier.
Unique identifier for the patient's household.
Age of the child in days.

All days of observation that fall within an acute lower respiratory Infection (ALRI) episode are coded with the ALRI episode count. ALRI episodes were separated by 14 ALRI-free days. Days when data are missing were classified as ALRI-free (if there were 14 missing days before a day with ALRI, the episode started on day with ALRI). Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether this day of observation marked the beginning of a acute lower respiratory infection (ALRI) episode or was within an ALRI episode. ALRI episodes were separated by 14 ALRI-free days. Days when data are missing were classified as ALRI-free (if there were 14 missing days before a day with ALRI, the episode started on day with ALRI). Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether the participant actively breastfed yesterday. Qualitative dietary diversity was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 8 months of age. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.
Participant's activity level, as reported by the mother. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Number of acute diarrheal episodes lasting less than 7 days.

Whether the participant's appetite decreased during this diarrheal episode. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Whether the mother reported any illness in the participant. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Participant's appetite, as reported by the mother. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Body Mass Index (BMI)-for-age z-score, measured in participants 24m and older. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.
Number of BCG vaccine doses received.

Whether blood in stool was reported at any time during this diarrheal episode. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether the mother reported any blood in the participant's stool. If the participant had blood in their stool, the participant was referred to the nearest health center for assessment and treatment. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether breastfeeding data was present today or not. Data were generally collected for yesterday's diet (ie, the day prior to the day the field worker is in the home). Breastfeeding surveillance occurred twice per week throughout 24 or 36 months of age. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.

Breastfeeding status was changed starting with the first occasion clear liquid, animal milk, formula, or solid food were given, and did not allow for back and forth. Breastfeeding surveillance occurred twice per week throughout 24 or 36 months of age. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.

Number of times breastfed during day the day of food recall. Quantitative dietary intake based on 24hr food recall was assessed on a monthly basis from 9-24 months of age, with an optional extension up to 60 months of age. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.

Number of times breastfed during day the day of food recall. Quantitative dietary intake based on 24hr food recall was assessed on a monthly basis from 9-24 months of age, with an optional extension up to 60 months of age. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.

Number of time breastfed during night the day of food recall. Quantitative dietary intake based on 24hr food recall was assessed on a monthly basis from 9-24 months of age, with an optional extension up to 60 months of age. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.

Number of times the participant was breastfed yesterday, from sunrise to sunset. Qualitative dietary diversity was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 8 months of age. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.

Number of times the participant was breastfed last night, from sunset to sunrise. Qualitative dietary diversity was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 8 months of age. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.

Cholera vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.

Feature: Combined Vesikari and Clark severity at episode
Definition: Combination of Vesikari and Clark severity scales, calculated by summing 'Diarrhea duration categorization,' 'Max loose stools per day categorization,' 'Days with vomiting at episode categorization,' 'Max dehydration categorization,' and 'Fever at episode categorization.' Possible values ranged from 1 (least severe) to 14 (most severe). Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Whether the mother reported the participant having a cough. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether presence of dairy (milk, formula, yogurt, cheese, or other dairy products) in the diet was reported at the last breastfeeding surveillance visit. Breastfeeding surveillance occurred twice per week throughout 24 or 36 months of age.

Whether this day of observation falls within an acute lower respiratory Infection (ALRI) episode. ALRI episodes were separated by 14 ALRI-free days. Days when data are missing were classified as ALRI-free (if there were 14 missing days before a day with ALRI, the episode started on day with ALRI). Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether this day of observation falls within a diarrheal episode. Diarrhea episodes were separated by two diarrhea-free days. Days when data were missing were classified as diarrhea-free (if there were two missing days before a day with diarrhea, the episode started on day with diarrhea). Days with Lactulose:Mannitol (L:M)-induced diarrhea were classified as diarrhea-free. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Duration of decreased appetite during an episode, measured in days.

Feature: Dehydrating diarrhea diagnosed by medical professional
Definition: Whether dehydrating diarrhea was diagnosed by a medical professional. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Categorization of the number of days this diarrheal episode lasted. Used to calculate 'Combined Vesikari and Clark severity at episode.' Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Feature: Diarrhea, ALRI, fever, or vomiting present
Definition: Whether diarrhea episode, acute lower respiratory infection (ALRI), cough, fever, or vomiting were present on that day of observation. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether the mother reported the participant having diarrhea. This variable was not used in the identification of diarrhea. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Duration of a diarrheal episode in days.

All days of observation that fall within a diarrheal episode were coded with the cumulative diarrheal episode count. Diarrhea episodes were separated by two diarrhea-free days. Days with missing data were classified as diarrhea-free (if there were two missing days before a day with diarrhea, the episode started on day with diarrhea). Days with Lactulose:Mannitol (L:M)-induced diarrhea were classified as diarrhea-free. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether this day of observation marked the beginning of a diarrheal episode or was within a diarrheal episode. Diarrhea episodes were separated by two diarrhea-free days. Days when data are missing were classified as diarrhea-free (if there were two missing days before a day with diarrhea, the episode started on day with diarrhea). Days with Lactulose:Mannitol (L:M)-induced diarrhea were classified as diarrhea-free. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether a stool sample was collected from the participant during the surveillance visit. Diarrheal stool samples were collected if the participant had diarrhea yesterday (>3 loose stools) AND if no diarrhea sample has been collected for this episode of diarrhea within the past two weeks. Only one diarrhea sample should be collected for each diarrhea episode or, in the case of persistent diarrhea, samples should be taken every two weeks during the extended episode. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Feature: Diphtheria, pertussis, and tetanus (DPT) vaccine dose number
Definition: Number of DPT vaccine doses received.

Feature: Ear pain or pulling, caregiver report
Definition: Whether the mother reported any ear pain or pulling by the participant. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether exclusive breastfeeding (breastfeeding present with no other liquids or solids) was reported at the last visit. Participants could switch back and forth between exclusive and different types of non-exclusive breastfeeding. Data between breastfeeding surveillance visits were filled in forward. Breastfeeding surveillance occurred twice per week throughout 24 or 36 months of age.

Whether the maximum temperature (Celsius) reported during this diarrheal episode was <37.5 (the Integrated Management of Childhood Illness (IMCI) fever definition). Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Maximum temperature (originally reported in Celsius) during this diarrheal episode. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Categorization of fever reported during the diarrheal episode. Used to calculate 'Combined Vesikari and Clark severity at episode.' Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Maximum recorded body temperature during a fever episode, in Celsius.
Whether the mother reported the participant having a fever. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether this was the first day of an acute lower respiratory Infection (ALRI) episode. ALRI episodes were separated by 14 ALRI-free days. Days when data are missing were classified as ALRI-free (if there were 14 missing days before a day with ALRI, the episode started on day with ALRI). Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether this is the first day of a diarrheal episode. Diarrhea episodes were separated by two diarrhea-free days. Days when data are missing were classified as diarrhea-free (if there were two missing days before a day with diarrhea, the episode started on day with diarrhea). Days with Lactulose:Mannitol (L:M)-induced diarrhea were classified as diarrhea-free. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Feature: Haemophilus influenzae type B vaccine dose number
Definition: Haemophilus influenzae type B vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.
Head circumference z-score. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.
Patient's height measured in centimeters.

Length-for-age or height-for-age z-score, as appropriate by age. Length used when younger than 24m, height used when older than 24m. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.

Hepatitis B vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.
Whether participant was hospitalized on this day. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Whether symptom data was available for this day of observation. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Number of Inactivated Poliovirus Vaccine doses received.

Influenza vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.

Length-for-age or height-for-age z-score, as appropriate by age. Length used when younger than 24m, height used when older than 24m. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.
Length-for-age z-score. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.

Feature: Loose stools in day count, caregiver report
Definition: Number of loose stools passed by the participant per day, as reported by the mother. If the participant passed 8 or more loose stools in a 24 hour period, the participant was referred to the nearest health center for assessment and treatment. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Maximum dehydration level during this diarrheal episode. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Feature: Maximum loose stools at episode count
Definition: Maximum number of loose stools that occurred on a single day during this diarrheal episode. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Feature: Mean bacteria count in 1st diarrheal stools
Definition: Mean number of bacteria species identified in first diarrheal stool samples per participant to date. Complete testing of stool samples is required to calculate this variable. Includes information from first diarrheal stools collected for each diarrheal episode. Whenever a participant produced at least 3 loose stools in a single day, diarrheal stool samples were collected before the end of the episode. Work up for detection of enteric pathogens was performed on the first stool sample collected during each diarrheal episode until 24 months of age (with a possible extension to 36 months of age).

Feature: Mean parasite count in 1st diarrheal stools
Definition: Mean number of parasite species identified in first diarrheal stool samples per participant to date. Complete testing of stool samples is required to calculate this variable. Includes information from first diarrheal stools collected for each diarrheal episode. Whenever a participant produced at least 3 loose stools in a single day, diarrheal stool samples were collected before the end of the episode. Work up for detection of enteric pathogens was performed on diarrheal stool samples collected until 24 months of age (with a possible extension to 36 months of age).

Feature: Mean pathogen (excluding NRV) count in 1st diarrheal stools
Definition: Average pathogen count (excluding Non-Rotavirus) in the first diarrheal stool sample.

Feature: Mean pathogen (excluding NRV) count in 1st monthly stools
Definition: Average pathogen count (excluding Non-Rotavirus) in the first monthly routine stool sample.

Feature: Mean pathogen (excluding NRV) count in stools
Definition: Average pathogen count (excluding Non-Rotavirus) across all stool samples.

Feature: Mean pathogen count in 1st diarrheal stools
Definition: Mean number of pathogen species identified in first diarrheal stool samples per participant to date. Complete testing of stool samples is required to calculate this variable. Includes information from first diarrheal stools collected for each diarrheal episode. Whenever a participant produced at least 3 loose stools in a single day, diarrheal stool samples were collected before the end of the episode. Work up for detection of enteric pathogens was performed on diarrheal stool samples collected until 24 months of age (with a possible extension to 36 months of age).

Feature: Mean pathogen count in 1st monthly stools
Definition: Mean number of pathogen species identified in first monthly asymptomatic stool samples per participant to date. Complete testing of stool samples is required to calculate this variable. Includes information from first monthly asymptomatic stools collected for each month. Work up for detection of enteric pathogens was performed on first monthly asymptomatic stool samples collected at 1-12, 15, 18, 21, and 24 months of age (with the option to test additional monthly stool samples to 36 months of age).

Mean number of pathogen species identified in stool samples per participant to date.Complete testing of stool samples is required to calculate this variable. Includes information from first monthly asymptomatic stools collected for each month and first diarrheal stools collected for each diarrheal episode. Whenever a participant produced at least 3 loose stools in a single day, diarrheal stool samples were collected before the end of the episode. Work up for detection of enteric pathogens was performed on diarrheal stool samples collected until 24 months of age (with a possible extension to 36 months of age) and on first monthly asymptomatic stool samples collected at 1-12, 15, 18, 21, and 24 months of age (with the option to test additional monthly stool samples to 36 months of age).

Feature: Mean virus (excluding NRV) count in 1st diarrheal stools
Definition: Average viral load (excluding Non-Rotavirus) in the first diarrheal stool sample.

Feature: Mean virus count in 1st diarrheal stools
Definition: Mean number of virus species identified in first diarrheal stool samples per participant to date. Complete testing of stool samples is required to calculate this variable. Includes information from first diarrheal stools collected for each diarrheal episode. Whenever a participant produced at least 3 loose stools in a single day, diarrheal stool samples were collected before the end of the episode. Work up for detection of enteric pathogens was performed on the first stool sample collected during each diarrheal episode until 24 months of age (with a possible extension to 36 months of age).

Measles vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.

Feature: Measles, mumps, and rubella (MMR) vaccine dose number
Definition: Number of MMR vaccine doses received.

Meningitis vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.

Whether the participant met the GEMS definition of moderate-to-severe diarrhea within -/+ 2 days of stool collection. Whenever a participant produced at least 3 loose stools in a single day, diarrheal stool samples were collected before the end of the episode. First monthly asymptomatic stool samples were collected every month from 0-24 months of age, with a possible extension to 36 months of age. First monthly asymptomatic stool samples collected at 1-12, 15, 18, 21, and 24 months of age were included in analyses of gut function.

Mumps vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.

Whether no breastfeeding was reported at the last visit. Participants could switch back and forth between exclusive and different types of non-exclusive breastfeeding. Data between breastfeeding surveillance visits were filled in forward. Breastfeeding surveillance occurred twice per week throughout 24 or 36 months of age.

Whether the mother reported that the participant was given oral rehydration salts (ORS), a pre-packaged ORS liquid, or a government-recommended homemade fluid for oral rehydration therapy (ORT). Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Number of Oral Poliovirus Vaccine doses received.

Feature: Other liquids (soup, broth, sugar water, carbonated drinks)
Definition: Frequency/amount of other liquids consumed by the child.

Whether partial breastfeeding (breastfeeding present with solids, animal milk (considering animal milk to be ingested with tea in Pakistan), and/or formula) was reported at the last visit. Participants could switch back and forth between exclusive and different types of non-exclusive breastfeeding. Data between breastfeeding surveillance visits were filled in forward. Breastfeeding surveillance occurred twice per week throughout 24 or 36 months of age.
Number of persistent diarrheal episodes lasting more than 14 days.
Whether the participant consumed any plain water yesterday. Qualitative dietary diversity was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 8 months of age.
Number of PCV doses received.

Whether predominant breastfeeding (breastfeeding and clear liquids (water, fruit juice, tea, other liquids) present, with no solids, animal milk (considering animal milk to be ingested with tea in Pakistan), or formula) was reported at the last visit. Participants could switch back and forth between exclusive and different types of non-exclusive breastfeeding. Data between breastfeeding surveillance visits were filled in forward. Breastfeeding surveillance occurred twice per week throughout 24 or 36 months of age.
Number of prolonged diarrheal episodes lasting between 7 and 14 days.

Rabies vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.

Feature: Recumbent length or height used for stature
Definition: Indicates whether length or height was used for 'Stature.' Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.

Whether the field worker referred the participant for medical care during the surveillance visit. The participant was referred to the nearest health center for assessment and treatment if: (i) their temperature was above 39C; (ii) they passed 8 or more loose stools in a 24 hour period; (iii) had blood in their stool; (iv) had severe dehydration; or (v) if the field worker determined that the participant had an acute lower respiratory infection (ALRI). Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Rotavirus vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.

Rubella vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.

Whether the mother reported the participant having a shortness of breath. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether the participant had an acute lower respiratory Infection (ALRI). Participants who had a cough or shortness of breath (today or yesterday) PLUS a rapid respiratory rate (defined as: >60 breaths/min in participants aged 0 to <2 months, >50 breaths/min in participants aged 2 to <12 months and >40 breaths/min in participants aged 12 months to three years) were determined to have ALRI. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Whether the participant had diarrhea on this day of observation, defined in the MAL-ED study as having more than 2 loose stools in the day or, if there was blood in the stool, having 1-2 loose stools in the day. Since Lactulose:Mannitol (L:M) causes loose stools, days with Lactulose:Mannitol (L:M)-induced diarrhea (where the participant met the definition of diarrhea but had an Lactulose:Mannitol (L:M) test on that day or the previous day) were not classified as study-defined diarrhea. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.

Tetanus vaccine dose #, based on a reconstructed history. Vaccination history was assessed every 3 months from 3-12 months of age and from 24-36 months of age. For more information about the information source for vaccinations, contact the provider.
Duration in days between consecutive diarrheal episodes.
Duration in days since the most recent diarrheal episode.

Feature: Time with vomiting at episode categorization
Definition: Categorization of the total number of days where vomiting occurred during this diarrheal episode. Used to calculate 'Combined Vesikari and Clark severity at episode.' Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Whether the mother reported any antibiotic usage by the participant. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age.
Duration of vomiting during an illness episode, in days.
Whether the mother reported any vomiting by the participant. Surveillance was conducted twice weekly for up to 3 years of age to assess illness on all days since the last surveillance visit.
Patient's body weight measured in kilograms.
Weight-for-age z-score. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.
Weight-for-height z-score. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.

Weight-for-length or weight-for-height z-score, as appropriate by age. Length used when younger than 24m, height used when older than 24m. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.
Weight-for-length z-score. Anthropometry was assessed on a monthly basis up to and including 36m of age, and subsequently every 3 months up to and including 60m of age.

Feature: Age 1st animal milk or formula given (days)
Definition: Age of the child when first introduced to animal milk or formula, in days.

Feature: Age 1st animal milk or solids given (days)
Definition: Age of the child when first introduced to animal milk or solid foods, in days.
Age of the child when first introduced to clear liquids, in days.
Age of the child when first introduced to solid foods, in days.

Feature: Age at 1st day of biweekly breastfeeding surveillance (days)
Definition: Study variable. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.
Study variable. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: The complementary feeding gap. Growth faltering often begins when low-quality solid foods replace nutrient-dense milk.
Age of the child at the conclusion of illness monitoring, in days.
Age of the child when exclusive breastfeeding ended, in days.
Weight of the child at birth, measured in kilograms.
Whether the participant began breastfeeding within 24 hours after birth. Baseline breastfeeding information was collected upon enrollment.

Feature: Exclusively breastfed at 1st day of surveillance
Definition: Whether the participant was exclusively breastfeeding on the 1st day of bi-weekly breastfeeding surveillance data collection. Baseline breastfeeding information was collected upon enrollment.
Whether the participant was fed the first milk (colostrum). Baseline breastfeeding information was collected upon enrollment.
Whether the participant was lost to follow-up and a non-continuation form was completed to collect information about the reason for leaving the study.

Whether the participant was randomly chosen to have their monthly stool samples tested for Norovirus. 10% of all participants followed for at least 6 months at each site were selected to have their monthly stools tested for Norovirus.
Whether there was prelacteal feeding of the participant. Baseline breastfeeding information was collected upon enrollment.
Biological sex of the patient.

How long after childbirth did the mother begin breastfeeding the participant. Baseline breastfeeding information was collected upon enrollment. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Nutritional and immunological proxy. Breastfeeding provides essential passive immunity against common enteric pathogens.

Whether the participant's mother had ever attended school. This variable refers to formal school only, and includes any formal schooling beginning with kindergarten. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Feature: Ate fewer meals due to lack of food last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether any member of the participant's household had to eat fewer meals in a day because there was not enough food. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.

Feature: Ate foods not wanted last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether any member of the participant's household had to eat some foods that they really did not want to eat because of a lack of resources to obtain other types of food. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.

Feature: Ate smaller meal than needed last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether any member of the participant's household had to eat a smaller meal than they felt they needed because there was not enough food. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.
Whether the household had a bank account. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.
Whether the household had a bed. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.
Whether the household had a chair. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Whether the household drinking water source was categorized as improved or unimproved, based on WHO definitions. Drinking water sources categorized as 'Improved' include water that is 'Piped into dwelling' or 'Piped into yard or plot,' or from a 'Public tap or stand pipe;' a 'Tube well or borehole;' or a 'Protected well.' Drinking water sources categorized as 'Unimproved' include water that is from an 'Unprotected well' or 'Surface water.' Other sources of drinking water were considered 'Improved,' except for Peru where it was considered 'Unimproved' due to site-specific circumstances. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

The main source of drinking water for members of the household. If the source varies by season, the main source used at the time of interview. Other sources of drinking water were considered 'Improved,' except for Peru where it was considered 'Unimproved' due to site-specific circumstances. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Average monthly income for the entire household, converted to US Dollars. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Average monthly income for the entire household, reported in the local currency. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Whether fewer than 2 people, on average, sleep in each room in the dwelling. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.
Observed main material of the floor. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Feature: Food security survey conducted in target window
Definition: Whether food security was assessed within a -/+ 15 day target window. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.

Feature: Had to eat a limited variety of foods last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether any member of the participant's household had to eat a limited variety of foods due to a lack of resources. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.
Measure of household food insecurity based on the HFIAS framework.

Feature: Household Food Insecurity Access Scale (HFIAS) score
Definition: Quantitative score representing the severity of household food insecurity.

Sum of 8 variables: (i) 'Bank account,' (ii) 'Bed,' (iii) 'Chair,' (iv) 'Table,' (v) 'Television,' (vi) 'Refrigerator,' (vii) 'Separate kitchen,' (vii) 'Fewer than 2 people per room.' Positive responses for each of these variables got a score of 1 while negative responses got a score of 0. Used as a component in calculating the WAMI index and ranged from 0 (low) to 8 (high). Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Average monthly income for the entire household, converted to US Dollars, categorized into all-site noniles. Used as a component in calculating the WAMI index. Values ranged from 0 (low) to 8 (high). Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.
Number of years of formal education completed by the patient's mother.

Years of schooling that the participant's mother had completed, divided by 2. Used as a component in calculating the WAMI index. Values ranged from 0 (low) to 8 (high). Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Mean number of people sleeping in each room in the dwelling. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months. | CLINICAL CONTEXT: Crowding index. High density is a proxy for poor hygiene and increased infectious disease transmission risk.

Feature: No food of any kind last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether there was ever no food to eat of any kind in the participant's household because of lack of resources to get food. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months. Note that for Brazil, the answer for this question is frequently '2 points - Sometimes,' instead of '0 points - No,' because the question was phrased as: 'In the past four weeks, whether some type of food of any kind was lacking in your household because of lack of resources to get food.'

Feature: Not able to eat preferred foods last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether any member of the participant's household was not able to eat the kinds of foods they preferred because of a lack of resources. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.

Sum of all 4 components used to calculate the WAMI index: (i) 'Sanitation and drinking water score,' (ii) 'Assets score,' (iii) 'Income score,' and (iv) 'Maternal education score.' Values ranged from 0 (low) to 32 (high). Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Number of people who usually sleep in this household on an average night. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.
Whether the household had a refrigerator. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.
Observed main material of the roof. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

The total number of rooms in the house including the veranda, the living and sleeping rooms, as well as attached kitchens used only by this household. It excludes storerooms and separate kitchens that are shared with other families/households. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Sum of sanitation and drinking water scores. Used as a component in calculating the WAMI index. Households with 'Drinking water categorization' and 'Sanitation categorization' variables classified as 'improved' got a score of 4 for each; 'unimproved' responses got a score of 0 for each. Values ranged from 0 (low) to 8 (high). Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Whether the household sanitation facilities were classified as improved or unimproved, based on WHO definitions. Sanitation facilities categorized as 'Improved' include those classified as 'Other,' 'Pit latrine without flush/with slab,''Flush to piped sewer system,' 'Flush to septic tank,' 'Flush to pit latrine,' or 'Flush to somewhere else.' 'No facility/bush/field or bucket toilet/pit latrine without slab' was considered 'Unimproved.' Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Whether the dwelling has a separate room which was used as a kitchen, in order to provide additional information on the hygiene status of the household. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Number of food/beverage items where dark green leafy vegetables was a central feature for this day of food recall. Quantitative dietary intake based on 24hr food recall was assessed on a monthly basis from 9-24 months of age, with an optional extension up to 60 months of age.

Feature: Target month for food security survey
Definition: Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.

Feature: Target month for socioeconomic status survey
Definition: Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.
Whether the household had a television. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Type of toilet or latrine facility usually used by members of the household. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

An index of socio-economic status that includes access to improved water and sanitation, eight selected assets, maternal education, and household income. The WAMI index was calculated by dividing the 'Overall socioeconomic score' by 32. Values ranged from 0 (low) to 1 (high). Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.
Observed main material of the walls. Socioeconomic status was assessed every 6 months from 6-24 months and again at 60 months of age, with optional assessments from 30-60 months.

Feature: Went 24 hours without food last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether any member of the participant's household had go a whole day and night without eating anything because there was not enough food. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.

Feature: Went to sleep hungry last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether any member of the participant's household had go to sleep at night hungry because there was not enough food. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.

Feature: Worried that there was not enough food last month
Definition: In the past four weeks, whether the participant's mother, primary caregiver, or head of household worried that the household would not have enough food. Food security was assessed every 6 months from 0-24 months, with optional assessments at 30 and 36 months.
